{{Quote|System Patrol Craft ''Glas'' hailing ''Victory''-class Star Destroyer ''Protector'', we formally request asylum.|Ripoblus refugees, to Admiral Harkov|audio=GlasAsylum-TF.flac|<ref name="TF" />}}
The '''''Glas''''' was an [[IPV-1 System Patrol Craft]] that was used by the [[Ripoblus government]] during the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial era]]. In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], it was hijacked by a group of [[Ripoblus (people)|Ripoblus]] [[Scientist/Legends|scientists]] who were growing disenchanted with the [[Sepan Civil War]], and wished to seek asylum with the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Since the ''Glas'' had no [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]], the [[Victory I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory I''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Protector (Victory I-class)|''Protector'']] arrived in the [[Sepan system/Legends|Sepan system]] to dock with the craft. Though the Ripoblus sent their own [[Starfighter/Legends|fighters]] to [[Second battle of the Sepan system|stop]] the scientists from escaping, the ''Glas'' docked successfully with the Imperial [[Warship/Legends|warship]], and was abandoned.

==Description==
As a [[Sienar Fleet Systems/Legends|Sienar Fleet Systems]] [[IPV-1 System Patrol Craft]],<ref name="XWA" /> the ''Glas'' was 150 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in length, and could reach speeds of up to eighty [[megalight]]s per [[Standard hour/Legends|hour]]. In terms of defense, its [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shield]] strength was at 150 [[SBD/Legends|SBDs]], with [[Hull/Legends|hull]] integrity at fifty [[RU/Legends|RU]]. The ''Glas'' was also armed with three [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannon]] turrets, and one rear-mounted [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]]. To the disadvantage of the [[Ripoblus (people)|Ripoblus]] [[Scientist/Legends|scientists]] who hijacked it during the [[Sepan Civil War]], it was not equipped with a [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]].<ref name="TF" />

==History==
The system patrol craft ''Glas'' was owned by the [[Ripoblus government]], and was in their service during the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial era]].<ref name="TF" /> In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="3ABY">{{TIEFighterDate}}</ref> during [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Harkov]]'s [[Sepan campaign|attempts]] to defuse the Sepan Civil War, a select group of scientists from [[Ripoblus/Legends|Ripoblus]] grew weary of the conflict and wished to distance themselves from it as much as possible. They were mainly dissatisfied with the manner in which their research was being used by their government, and no longer wished to contribute to the war. Hijacking the ''Glas'' and the [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttles]] [[Nazaar|''Nazaar 1'']] and ''Nazaar 2'', the scientists sought refuge with the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] forces in the [[Sepan sector/Legends|sector]]. As the ''Glas'' had no hyperdrive system, Harkov's [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[Victory I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory I''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Protector (Victory I-class)|''Protector'']], journeyed to its location. The scientists arranged to have the ''Glas'' dock with the ''Protector'' to allow a transfer of personnel, but before the stolen patrol craft could reach the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]], [[Starfighter/Legends|fighters]] sent by the Ripoblus government [[Second battle of the Sepan system|entered]] the [[Sepan system/Legends|system]].<ref name="TF" />

[[File:GlasProtector-TF.png|thumb|left|260px|The ''Glas'' approaches the ''Protector'']]
As Ripoblus [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|BTL Y-wing starfighters]] from [[Able Squadron]], as well as the [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|''Lambda''-class T-4a shuttles]] of [[Lambda Group]] bore down on the ''Glas'', the ''Protector'' launched [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE/IN interceptors]] to fend them off. Eventually, the [[Dimok (people)|Dimoks]], enemies of the Ripoblus, sent their own fighters to attack the ''Glas''. Their attack force mirrored that of the Ripoblus, as they dispatched Y-wings from [[Baker Squadron]] and shuttles from [[Sigma group]]. The Dimoks were also intercepted by the Imperial fighters, one of which was piloted by [[Ace/Legends|ace]] [[Maarek Stele/Legends|Maarek Stele]]. The ''Glas'' successfully docked with the ''Protector'', transferring the scientists, while placing a set of decoys aboard the [[Starship/Legends|starship]]. Abandoned, the patrol craft was set on [[Autopilot/Legends|autopilot]] and sent out into [[Realspace/Legends|space]], though it was still considered a potential target by the enemy. [[Delta-class JV-7 escort shuttle|''Delta''-class JV-7 escort shuttles]] from the Ripoblus arrived, but they were far too late to capture the scientists. Meanwhile, ''Nazaar 1'' and ''Nazaar 2'' successfully entered the [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] of the Imperial [[Warship/Legends|warship]]. Due to his role in protecting the ''Glas'', Stele was given a promotion to [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]].<ref name="TF:TOSG">''[[TIE Fighter: The Official Strategy Guide]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The ''Glas'' first appeared in [[Totally Games]]'s [[1994]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|''Star Wars: TIE Fighter'']].<ref name="TF" /> The ship makes its only appearance in "Rescue War Refugees," the third mission of the game's second tour of duty, in which the player, in the role of Maarek Stele, is tasked with protecting the ''Glas'' until it finishes docking with the ''Protector''.<ref name="TF:TOSG" />
